items.description: «Кіевская Старина» — щомісячний історико-етнографічний та літературний часопис. Видавався у Києві російською та українською мовами упродовж 1882-1907 років (останній рік — українською під назвою «Україна»). Дозвіл цензури на вихід першого випуску отримано 1 лютого 1882. Часопис відіграв велику роль у розвитку української культури в умовах Російської імперії. З журналом співпрацювали видатні істори

items.description: The Boy’s Own Paper was a British weekly paper containing stories, articles and inspirational features, aimed at young and teenage boys.   It was launched in 1879 and remained in publication until 1967.   Issues generally included adventure stories or sports-related stories (especially cricket), craft instructions, sports, games, nature articles, historical and biographical pieces, puzzles, etc. B

items.description: Railway Conductors Monthly magazine Official organ of the Order of Railway Conductors of America (ORC). C.S. Wheaton, editor. Volume 1 (Jan.-Dec. 1884), complete run of 12 monthly issues. Includes index. Individual issues are bookmarked as part of this pdf. Scanned by Google as part of the Google Books project. Published in the USA prior to 1923, public domain

items.description: The Century Magazine began as Scribner’s Monthly in 1870, and became Century Magazine in 1881. It brought readers a wide range of general-interest articles on famous people (contemporary and historic), history, archaeology, science, the latest discoveries, world travel and cultures, and discussions of current political issues and controversies.   The Century Magazine serialized numerous key works

Published anonymously, but written by Ernest Alfred Vizetelly (1853-1922), the son of publisher Henry Vizetelly.
